pi_items.source: Synthetic domain created in setup_task.sh for testing; matches task.json metadata
SpamAssassin documentation confirms required_score is a valid configuration parameter; value of 4 is lower than default 5.0 but within acceptable range
SpamAssassin documentation confirms spam_delete_level is valid; value of 10 is appropriately higher than required_score of 4, matching best practices
Synthetic email domain; web search found no evidence of real trustedpartner.org domain; format matches SpamAssassin whitelist_from syntax
Synthetic email address; web search indicates important-alerts.net is associated with phishing campaigns, not legitimate service; format matches SpamAssassin whitelist_from syntax
Synthetic domain using .example reserved TLD for documentation; format matches SpamAssassin blacklist_from syntax
Synthetic email using .example reserved TLD for documentation; format matches SpamAssassin blacklist_from syntax

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data for testing SpamAssassin spam filtering configuration in Virtualmin. Target domain is greenfield.test (fictional test domain). Configuration thresholds are valid: required_score=4 (below SpamAssassin default of 5.0, appropriate for aggressive filtering) and delete_score=10 (appropriately higher than classification threshold). All whitelist and blacklist entries use proper SpamAssassin syntax with wildcard patterns and specific email addresses, but use synthetic/fictional domains: trustedpartner.org (non-existent), important-alerts.net (associated with phishing), known-spammer.example (reserved .example TLD), and bulkmail.example (reserved .example TLD).
